LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S MOTION TO RECUSE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING SOME OF THE TESTIMONY OF PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL MAY BE APPROPRIATE, THE COURT IS NOT INCLINED TO DEPRIVE PLAINTIFFS OF THEIR CHOSEN COUNSEL IN LIGHT OF THEIR CERTIFICATION UNDER OATH IN THEIR DECLARATIONS THAT THEY WANT TO KEEP CURRENT COUNSEL NOTWITHSTANDING THE POSSIBILITY THAT COUNSEL MAY BECOME A WITNESS. IT WILL BE LEFT TO FUTURE PROCEEDINGS TO DETERMINE WHAT DISCOVERY OR TESTIMONY MAY PROPERLY BE ELICITED FROM COUNSEL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicolet Corliss, Esq., 415.433.4800, for plaintiffs Mt. Tam Laser and Skin Care Corporation, Karol Meier and Sandi Selig-Farney.
Paul Frassetto, Esq., 415.354.2700, for defendant Ronald P. Goldman, A Professional Law Corporation DBA Goldman Law Firm.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION, KAROL MEIER and SANDI SELIG-FARNEY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is denied. Judgment was entered on April 16, 2014, and the Court lacks the authority to grant leave to amend because the judgment has not been vacated. See Young v. Berry Equipment Rentals, Inc. (1976) 55 Cal.App.3d 35, 38 ("Amendments proffered after judgment is rendered, however, are allowed only if the judgment is vacated as by granting a motion for new trial."); LaForge v. Groendyke (1952) 108 Cal.App.2d 522, 525 ("Ordinarily amendments after judgment are allowed only on an application for relief, and after the judgment has been vacated."). The Court denies defendant's request for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.5 because that statute does not apply to this action. Plaintiff filed this action on June 29, 2012, and CCP 128.5 only applies to actions that were commenced prior to December 31, 1994. See CCP 128.5(b)(1). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.

Ntc Of Mtn For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Pltffs
Off Calendar Jan-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S MOTION For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO SEEK THE REQUESTED RELIEF FROM THE TRIAL DEPARTMENT. Plaintiffs Meier and Selig-Farney served further verified responses to Form Interrogatories 6.2 and 6.3 on or about December 6, 2013. The responses were all hedged with the proviso that the manifestations identified in response to Form Interrogatories 6.2 and 6.3 "include but are not necessary limited to loss of sleep, anxiety, headaches, and stomach discomfort." Plaintiffs' responses to Form Interrogatory 6.2 are evasive and incomplete because they do not identify each injury. Plaintiffs' responses to Form Interrogatory 6.3 are evasive and incomplete because the interrogatory asks for information about each complaint that the Plaintiffs' attribute to the incident. However, given that Defendant had not deposed Plaintiffs prior to the date of the amended verified responses, and the discovery cutoff appears to have been extended in connection with the Court's continuance of the trial date to February 24, 2013, Defendant still has the opportunity to elicit, during Plaintiffs' depositions, further information about the manifestations Plaintiffs attribute to their emotional distress. Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Appearances: Gregory R. Shaughnessy of the Law Offices of Gregory R. Shaughnessy (415.435.2409) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party; Jack V. Valinoti of Bradshaw & Associates (415.433.4800) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the first and fourth causes of action in Plaintiffs' Complaint is denied. Plaintiffs' and Defendant's requests for judicial notice are granted. Plaintiffs have alleged sufficient facts to state a claim for legal malpractice and breach of fiduciary duty. (See Complaint 44-64, 78-81; Coscia v. McKenna & Cuneo (2001) 25 Cal.4th 1194, 1199.) Taking the facts as true, but for Defendant's breaches of duty, Plaintiffs would not have been exposed to additional liability in the Killips matter and would not have incurred attorney's fees and costs defending the additional claims. (See Complaint 57-59.) It is a factual question whether Plaintiffs could have achieved a better result in the underlying action but for Defendant's alleged breach of duty. (See Lazy Acres Market v. Tseng 152 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1435 (2007).) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFFS MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION, KAROL MEIER and SANDI SELIG-FARNEY'S Motion For Evidentiary, Issue, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions is Denied without prejudice. The Court denies requested relief of evidentiary, issue, terminating and monetary sanctions for asserted non-compliance with February 26, 2013 discovery order. The Court cannot determine, based on the moving papers, what issues were presented to Pro Tem Judge Kane that formed the basis of his February 26, 2013 order. The Court cannot "chang[e] the word 'prior' to 'after' [to] harmonize and make meaningful all of the provisions of the February 26, 2013 order," as requested by Plaintiffs in their reply papers. Parties may, but are not required, to bring the matter to Pro Tem Judge Kane for clarification on a date that he is scheduled to hear discovery matters. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
